name: Fillets (or Steaks) of Walleye Mornay
recipeIngredient: Pinch of Cayenne OR dash of hot sauce
recipeInstructions:
1. Place fillets or steaks in a saucepan, just cover with cold water. Add salt, lemon juice, and cayenne. Bring to a simmer.
2. Turn down the heat and simmer for 10-12 minutes. Remove fish but retain the fish broth.
3. Place fish in a buttered baking dish.
4. Stir together 1 cup fish broth and the cream.
5. Melt 3 tablespoons of butter and add minced onion, tarragon or marjoram. Cook until onions are soft.
6. Stir in flour and cook for a minute or two. Turn off heat.
7. Stir in 2 cups of the fish broth until smooth. Return to low heat, stirring until sauce begins to thicken.
8. Spoon 2-3 tablespoons of the sauce into the beaten eggs, then stir the beaten eggs slowly into the sauce. Add cheese, stirring continuously and cook until uniform.
9. Pour the sauce over the fish, dot with remaining butter, and bake in a preheated oven at 375°F for about 10 minutes to brown the sauce.
